skill.name: jaspar-api
skill.description: Access JASPAR database for transcription factor binding profiles (matrices), collections, and species via REST API. Use when user wants to search for transcription factors, retrieve matrix details (PFM/PWM), infer profiles from protein sequences, or explore JASPAR collections.
skill.summary: Query the JASPAR database to search transcription factor binding profiles and retrieve matrix data through a REST API.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 102 static analysis findings are false positives when evaluated in context. The skill is a legitimate bioinformatics API client for the JASPAR database. It makes HTTP requests to jaspar.elixir.no (a well-known scientific database) and uses markdown code blocks in documentation to show script usage examples. No cryptographic operations exist in the codebase. No prompt injection or malicious intent was detected.

low_findings.title: Network access to JASPAR API - legitimate use
low_findings.description: The Python client uses urllib.request to make HTTP requests to jaspar.elixir.no, the official JASPAR database API. This is the declared purpose of the skill. No unauthorized or suspicious endpoints are accessed.

low_findings.title: Shell command examples in documentation - false positive
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains bash code blocks showing how to run the included Python script (e.g., python scripts/jaspar_client.py). These are usage instructions for the AI, not arbitrary command execution. No user input is passed to shell commands.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm keyword false match
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged keywords such as 'security' in JSON field names, 'CORE' as a collection name, and other terms as weak cryptographic algorithm usage. No actual cryptographic operations exist in any file. This is a scanner false positive.

low_findings.title: System and network reconnaissance keyword false match
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged field names like 'practice_boundaries', 'methodological_ground', and API parameter documentation as reconnaissance indicators. These are JSON data field names and API parameter documentation, not active reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Search transcription factor data from the JASPAR database
content.value_statement: Researchers need a reliable way to access transcription factor binding profile data from the JASPAR database. This skill provides a structured method to search for matrix profiles, collections, and species data through the JASPAR REST API.
actual_capabilities: Search for transcription factor binding profiles by name, taxonomic group, or TF class
Retrieve detailed matrix information including PFM (position frequency matrix) and PWM (position weight matrix) data
Infer matrix profiles from protein sequences
Explore JASPAR collections such as CORE and CNE
Browse and filter species data by taxonomic ID with pagination support
Filter results by release version, collection, and page size
limitations: Requires internet access to reach the JASPAR API at jaspar.elixir.no
Cannot modify or upload data to the JASPAR database (read-only access)
Protein sequence inference quality depends on the JASPAR database content, which may not cover all proteins
Does not provide visualization of binding profiles or motif logos

use_cases.title: Retrieve transcription factor binding profiles
use_cases.description: A molecular biologist searches for SMAD3 binding profiles in vertebrates to study TGF-beta signaling pathways.

use_cases.title: Explore JASPAR collections for research
use_cases.description: A bioinformatics student lists all available JASPAR collections and retrieves species data for a genomics course project.

use_cases.title: Infer binding profiles from protein sequences
use_cases.description: A computational biologist provides a protein sequence to infer potential transcription factor binding profiles for a gene regulation study.

prompt_templates.title: Search for transcription factor binding profiles
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for SMAD3 transcription factor binding profiles in vertebrates using the JASPAR database. List the top 5 results with their matrix IDs.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher wants to find transcription factor binding data for a specific protein.

prompt_templates.title: Get detailed matrix information
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve the full details for JASPAR matrix MA0001.1. Include the PFM, PWM, and species information in the output.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs detailed binding profile information for a known matrix ID.

prompt_templates.title: Infer binding profile from protein sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the JASPAR API, infer the matrix profile for this protein sequence: MSTAVLENPGLGRKLSDFGQETSYIED. Return any matching profiles found.
prompt_templates.scenario: A scientist has a protein sequence and wants to find its transcription factor binding characteristics.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive collection exploration
prompt_templates.prompt: List all available JASPAR collections and then find all species with tax ID 9606 (human). For each collection, show the number of available matrices.
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user wants to survey available data before running detailed searches.

output_examples.input: Search for SMAD3 transcription factor binding profiles in vertebrates.
output_examples.output: The skill queries the JASPAR API and returns a formatted list of matching matrix profiles. Each entry includes the matrix ID, name, collection, species, and taxonomic group. The user can then request full details for any specific matrix.

output_examples.input: Get details for matrix MA0001.1 from the JASPAR database.
output_examples.output: The skill returns the complete matrix record including the position frequency matrix (PFM), species information, collection, TF class and family, and a link to the full JASPAR entry. This data is formatted for easy inspection.

output_examples.input: List all available JASPAR collections and their species.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a structured overview of JASPAR collections (CORE, CNE, etc.) with species counts per collection. Each species entry includes the common name, taxonomic ID, and number of associated matrix profiles.

best_practices: Specify a taxonomic group or TF class when searching to narrow results and reduce API response time.
Use specific matrix IDs when retrieving details to ensure reproducible and verifiable results.
Verify internet connectivity before running the JASPAR API client to avoid timeout errors.
anti_patterns: Do not use generic search terms without filters - results will be too broad and may exceed API limits.
Do not assume the JASPAR API is available offline or without network access.
Do not modify the Python client script parameters without testing, as this may produce invalid API requests.

faq.question: What is the JASPAR database?
faq.answer: JASPAR is an open-access database of transcription factor binding profiles. It stores position frequency matrices (PFMs) and position weight matrices (PWMs) for transcription factors across multiple species.

faq.question: What types of data can I access with this skill?
faq.answer: You can search and retrieve transcription factor binding profiles (matrices), list JASPAR collections and species, and infer matrix profiles from protein sequences.

faq.question: Does this skill require an internet connection?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill makes HTTP requests to the JASPAR REST API at jaspar.elixir.no. An active internet connection is required.

faq.question: What is a transcription factor binding profile?
faq.answer: A transcription factor binding profile describes the DNA binding preferences of a transcription factor. It is typically stored as a position frequency matrix (PFM) or position weight matrix (PWM).

faq.question: Can I add or upload data to JASPAR using this skill?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides read-only access to the JASPAR database. You cannot modify, add, or upload data.

faq.question: Which JASPAR collections are available?
faq.answer: The skill supports all JASPAR collections including CORE (curated), CNE (Cis-Regulatory Non-coding Elements), and others. Use the collections list command to see all available collections.
